## Summary

Creates a fresh PR to replace the dead/no-op PR #1022 and capture the remaining Revise governance + execution work.

This adds `docs/governance/revise-volume-vii-execution-map.md`, which translates Volume VII Revise governance into concrete enforcement points.

## Context

Sister exposed the core maturity gap:

- Evaluation governance is mature.
- Revise governance and queue admission need the same rigor.
- Blocking bad cards is not enough; cards must regenerate, re-score, and independently earn user-facing admission.

The prior regeneration state-loop fix is already on `main` at `c5a943cb`, but remaining work still needs clean follow-through:

- Workbench queue must call the Volume VII admission gate.
- TrustedPath must consume only admission-passed cards.
- Final Review must ignore blocked/non-admitted cards.
- Telemetry must remain privacy-safe and reason-code based.

## This PR adds

- A canonical execution map for Volume VII wiring.
- The user-facing admission contract.
- Admin-only treatment of blocked cards.
- Candidate A/B/C role doctrine.
- Regeneration doctrine.
- Remaining enforcement tasks.
- Acceptance tests required before this work can be considered complete.
- Build order for the next code patch.

## Follow-up implementation checklist

- [ ] Wire `lib/revision/workbenchQueue.ts` through `runWorkbenchAdmissionGate(...)`.
- [ ] Add/update `__tests__/lib/revision/workbenchQueue.test.ts`.
- [ ] Wire TrustedPath to consume admission-passed cards only.
- [ ] Wire Final Review to exclude blocked/non-admitted cards.
- [ ] Add telemetry privacy assertions.
- [ ] Run `npx tsc --noEmit`.
- [ ] Run targeted revision tests.

## Product rule

A Revise card is not user-facing because it exists in a ledger.

A Revise card is user-facing only because it independently earns admission under Volume VII.
